trans-unit.source: Exercise 3: Assessing Accessibility to Vaccination Services
trans-unit.target: Exercise 3: Assessing Accessibility to Vaccination Services

trans-unit.source: Background
trans-unit.target: Background

trans-unit.source: Following your analysis of measles incidence rates per district, the Ministry of Health now wants to identify which populations have limited access to vaccination services. In this exercise, you will learn a simplified approach on how to model accessibility and determine how many people live **beyond a reasonable travel distance** from a vaccination point.
trans-unit.target: Following your analysis of measles incidence rates per district, the Ministry of Health now wants to identify which populations have limited access to vaccination services. In this exercise, you will learn a simplified approach on how to model accessibility and determine how many people live **beyond a reasonable travel distance** from a vaccination point.

trans-unit.source: Accessibility to health services is a key determinant of vaccination coverage. In many rural parts of Chad, communities may not have access to motorised transport and rely on walking or infrequent public transport. Thus, distance alone is not always a good measure — **travel time** provides a more realistic assessment.
trans-unit.target: Accessibility to health services is a key determinant of vaccination coverage. In many rural parts of Chad, communities may not have access to motorised transport and rely on walking or infrequent public transport. Thus, distance alone is not always a good measure — **travel time** provides a more realistic assessment.

trans-unit.source: Available Data
trans-unit.target: Available Data

trans-unit.source: Dataset name
trans-unit.target: Dataset name

trans-unit.source: Description
trans-unit.target: Description

trans-unit.source: Source
trans-unit.target: Source

trans-unit.source: Base project with administrative boundaries, health facility capacities, and incidence rates
trans-unit.target: Base project with administrative boundaries, health facility capacities, and incidence rates

trans-unit.source: 2025 population estimate per grid-cell
trans-unit.target: 2025 population estimate per grid-cell

trans-unit.source: [WorldPop](https://hub.worldpop.org/geodata/summary?id=72895)
trans-unit.target: [WorldPop](https://hub.worldpop.org/geodata/summary?id=72895)

trans-unit.source: Vaccination points (healthsites with cold chain)
trans-unit.target: Vaccination points (healthsites with cold chain)

trans-unit.source: Derived in Exercise 1
trans-unit.target: Derived in Exercise 1

trans-unit.source: Administrative boundaries (district level)
trans-unit.target: Administrative boundaries (district level)

trans-unit.source: [HDX – OCHA](https://data.humdata.org/dataset/cod-ab-tcd)
trans-unit.target: [HDX – OCHA](https://data.humdata.org/dataset/cod-ab-tcd)

trans-unit.source: `tcd_roads_ocha.shp` (Lines)
trans-unit.target: `tcd_roads_ocha.shp` (Lines)

trans-unit.source: Chad - Road Network
trans-unit.target: Chad - Road Network

trans-unit.source: [HDX - OCHA](https://data.humdata.org/dataset/chad-roads-osm-ministry-of-transport)
trans-unit.target: [HDX - OCHA](https://data.humdata.org/dataset/chad-roads-osm-ministry-of-transport)

trans-unit.source: Scenario
trans-unit.target: Scenario

trans-unit.source: You have identified a number of health facilities equipped with a cold chain for vaccine storage. The goal now is to calculate how much of the population is **within reasonable reach** of these facilities, and how many people live **beyond the accessible range**.
trans-unit.target: You have identified a number of health facilities equipped with a cold chain for vaccine storage. The goal now is to calculate how much of the population is **within reasonable reach** of these facilities, and how many people live **beyond the accessible range**.

trans-unit.source: To do this, you will generate travel-time surfaces around vaccination points and then use WorldPop data to estimate the population covered within each accessibility zone. This approach simplifies reality but provides a useful approximation of service coverage.
trans-unit.target: To do this, you will generate travel-time surfaces around vaccination points and then use WorldPop data to estimate the population covered within each accessibility zone. This approach simplifies reality but provides a useful approximation of service coverage.

trans-unit.source: Task 1: Opening the Project and Preparing the Data
trans-unit.target: Task 1: Opening the Project and Preparing the Data

trans-unit.source: Open your QGIS project from **Exercise 2**.
trans-unit.target: Open your QGIS project from **Exercise 2**.

trans-unit.source: Verify that the following layers are loaded:
trans-unit.target: Verify that the following layers are loaded:

trans-unit.source: `tcd_admbnda_adm2_20250212_AB.shp` (district boundaries)
trans-unit.target: `tcd_admbnda_adm2_20250212_AB.shp` (district boundaries)

trans-unit.source: `tcd_healthsites_points_capacities.gpkg` (vaccination points)
trans-unit.target: `tcd_healthsites_points_capacities.gpkg` (vaccination points)

trans-unit.source: `tcd_pop_2025_CN_100m_R2025A_v1.tif` (population raster)
trans-unit.target: `tcd_pop_2025_CN_100m_R2025A_v1.tif` (population raster)

trans-unit.source: `tcd_roads_ocha.shp` (road network)
trans-unit.target: `tcd_roads_ocha.shp` (road network)

trans-unit.source: Now we need to filter the `tcd_healthsites_points_capacities` layer to only include healthsites with a cold chain for vaccine storage. We will do so by opening the "Attribute table" and clicking on "Select features using an expression" ![](/fig/mActionSelectbyExpression.png)
trans-unit.target: Now we need to filter the `tcd_healthsites_points_capacities` layer to only include healthsites with a cold chain for vaccine storage. We will do so by opening the "Attribute table" and clicking on "Select features using an expression" ![](/fig/mActionSelectbyExpression.png)

trans-unit.source: Now click on the "Fields and Values" section and double-click on cold_chain
trans-unit.target: Now click on the "Fields and Values" section and double-click on cold_chain

trans-unit.source: Now right click on the `tcd_healthsites_points_capacities` → `Export` → `Save Selected Features As...`
trans-unit.target: Now right click on the `tcd_healthsites_points_capacities` → `Export` → `Save Selected Features As...`

trans-unit.source: Save it as a "Geopackage" in your `/data/interim/`-folder. Use a name like `tcd_cold_chain_healthsites_points_capacities`. Now we have only the healthsites that also provide a cold chain for vaccine storage and could be considered for a vaccination campaign.
trans-unit.target: Save it as a "Geopackage" in your `/data/interim/`-folder. Use a name like `tcd_cold_chain_healthsites_points_capacities`. Now we have only the healthsites that also provide a cold chain for vaccine storage and could be considered for a vaccination campaign.

trans-unit.source: Task 2: Creating Accessibility Area Around Vaccination Points
trans-unit.target: Task 2: Creating Accessibility Area Around Vaccination Points

trans-unit.source: Open the "Service area (from layer)" tool in the Processing Toolbox.
trans-unit.target: Open the "Service area (from layer)" tool in the Processing Toolbox.

trans-unit.source: Select the following parameters to run a Network Analysis. The algorithm creates a new vector with all the edges or parts of edges of a network line layer that can be reached within a distance or a time, starting from features of a point layer.
trans-unit.target: Select the following parameters to run a Network Analysis. The algorithm creates a new vector with all the edges or parts of edges of a network line layer that can be reached within a distance or a time, starting from features of a point layer.

trans-unit.source: For the Vector layer representing network select the Chad road network `tcd_roads_ocha`
trans-unit.target: For the Vector layer representing network select the Chad road network `tcd_roads_ocha`

trans-unit.source: Path type to calculate should be `Fastest`, as we want to work with travel time
trans-unit.target: Path type to calculate should be `Fastest`, as we want to work with travel time

trans-unit.source: Vector layer with start points is the newly created `tcd_cold_chain_healthsites_points_capacities`
trans-unit.target: Vector layer with start points is the newly created `tcd_cold_chain_healthsites_points_capacities`

trans-unit.source: Travel cost will be `2` as the information for the fastest path type is given in hours. So 2 will correspond to 2 hours of travel time
trans-unit.target: Travel cost will be `2` as the information for the fastest path type is given in hours. So 2 will correspond to 2 hours of travel time

trans-unit.source: The speed will be left at the Default speed of 50 km/h
trans-unit.target: The speed will be left at the Default speed of 50 km/h

trans-unit.source: The output will be called `Service area (lines)` and will include the road network accessible from a given healthsite within 2 hours of travel time at a travel speed of 50 km/h. To further process this data we need to reproject it to a metric CRS that depicts Chad without distorting too much. Select __EPSG: 102022__ and reproject the `Service area (lines)`. The output layer will be called `Reprojected`.
trans-unit.target: The output will be called `Service area (lines)` and will include the road network accessible from a given healthsite within 2 hours of travel time at a travel speed of 50 km/h. To further process this data we need to reproject it to a metric CRS that depicts Chad without distorting too much. Select __EPSG: 102022__ and reproject the `Service area (lines)`. The output layer will be called `Reprojected`.

trans-unit.source: To produce a more realistic representation of the accessible area around each vaccination point, we will buffer the `Service area (lines)` layer. Before buffering, we first need to [`Dissolve`](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_geoprocessing_wiki.html#dissolve) the service-area lines so they form a single unified geometry.
trans-unit.target: To produce a more realistic representation of the accessible area around each vaccination point, we will buffer the `Service area (lines)` layer. Before buffering, we first need to [`Dissolve`](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_geoprocessing_wiki.html#dissolve) the service-area lines so they form a single unified geometry.

trans-unit.source: Open the `Dissolve` tool and use the reprojected output as the __Input layer__
trans-unit.target: Open the `Dissolve` tool and use the reprojected output as the __Input layer__

trans-unit.source: In Dissolve fields, we won't select anything
trans-unit.target: In Dissolve fields, we won't select anything

trans-unit.source: Now we can buffer the Dissolved Service area lines by 2 km, which corresponds to around 30 minutes of walking.
trans-unit.target: Now we can buffer the Dissolved Service area lines by 2 km, which corresponds to around 30 minutes of walking.

trans-unit.source: Input layer will be the result of the dissolving process (likely called `Dissolved`) :::{figure} /fig/pub_health_chad_buffer.png
trans-unit.target: Input layer will be the result of the dissolving process (likely called `Dissolved`) :::{figure} /fig/pub_health_chad_buffer.png

source.ph: name: Buffer operation service area roads

target.ph: name: Buffer operation service area roads

trans-unit.source: Set the buffer distance to 2 km. The output should look similar to the screenshot below: the green area represents the estimated 2-hour accessibility zone around the vaccination points along the road network. :::{figure} /fig/en_3.40_m3_ex_3_access_vaccination.png
trans-unit.target: Set the buffer distance to 2 km. The output should look similar to the screenshot below: the green area represents the estimated 2-hour accessibility zone around the vaccination points along the road network. :::{figure} /fig/en_3.40_m3_ex_3_access_vaccination.png

trans-unit.source: Save the buffered service area by <kbd>right-clicking</kbd> on it and selecting `Make permament...`. Select "Geopackage" as the output format and save the layer to the `data/interim/`-folder and enter a file name such as `access_vaccination_points`. Click `Save`.
trans-unit.target: Save the buffered service area by <kbd>right-clicking</kbd> on it and selecting `Make permament...`. Select "Geopackage" as the output format and save the layer to the `data/interim/`-folder and enter a file name such as `access_vaccination_points`. Click `Save`.

trans-unit.source: Task 3: Calculating Population Coverage Using WorldPop
trans-unit.target: Task 3: Calculating Population Coverage Using WorldPop

trans-unit.source: Next, we will generate several population statistics. First, use the admin boundaries together with the WorldPop raster to calculate the total population for each district.
trans-unit.target: Next, we will generate several population statistics. First, use the admin boundaries together with the WorldPop raster to calculate the total population for each district.

trans-unit.source: In the **Processing Toolbox**, search for the tool **Zonal Statistics** and open it.
trans-unit.target: In the **Processing Toolbox**, search for the tool **Zonal Statistics** and open it.

trans-unit.source: **Input layer:** `tcd_admbnda_adm2_20250212_AB.shp`
trans-unit.target: **Input layer:** `tcd_admbnda_adm2_20250212_AB.shp`

trans-unit.source: **Raster layer:** `tcd_pop_2025_CN_100m_R2025A_v1.tif`
trans-unit.target: **Raster layer:** `tcd_pop_2025_CN_100m_R2025A_v1.tif`

trans-unit.source: **Raster band:** only 1 possible selection
trans-unit.target: **Raster band:** only 1 possible selection

trans-unit.source: **Output column prefix:** `pop_total_`
trans-unit.target: **Output column prefix:** `pop_total_`

trans-unit.source: **Statistics ot calculate:** `Sum`
trans-unit.target: **Statistics ot calculate:** `Sum`

trans-unit.source: **Output file name:** `pop_total_adm2`
trans-unit.target: **Output file name:** `pop_total_adm2`

trans-unit.source: Then, run an `Intersection` between the district boundaries with the total population information, and the service-area polygon. This step splits the single accessibility area into separate pieces that align with the district boundaries.
trans-unit.target: Then, run an `Intersection` between the district boundaries with the total population information, and the service-area polygon. This step splits the single accessibility area into separate pieces that align with the district boundaries.

trans-unit.source: In the **Processing Toolbox**, search for the tool **Intersection** and open it.
trans-unit.target: In the **Processing Toolbox**, search for the tool **Intersection** and open it.

trans-unit.source: **Input layer:** `pop_total_adm2`
trans-unit.target: **Input layer:** `pop_total_adm2`

trans-unit.source: **Overlay layer:** `access_vaccination_points`
trans-unit.target: **Overlay layer:** `access_vaccination_points`

trans-unit.source: **Output file name:** `adm2_access_vaccination_points`
trans-unit.target: **Output file name:** `adm2_access_vaccination_points`

trans-unit.source: Then run the algorithm. The output will be the access-to-vaccination geometry with the district information added. In other words, the single access area is split into multiple parts where it intersects district boundaries, assigning each portion to the corresponding district.
trans-unit.target: Then run the algorithm. The output will be the access-to-vaccination geometry with the district information added. In other words, the single access area is split into multiple parts where it intersects district boundaries, assigning each portion to the corresponding district.

trans-unit.source: With these intersected geometries, we can then calculate how many people fall within the 2-hour access zone across all districts, providing an estimate of district-level population coverage for vaccination services. ::::{grid} 2
trans-unit.target: With these intersected geometries, we can then calculate how many people fall within the 2-hour access zone across all districts, providing an estimate of district-level population coverage for vaccination services. ::::{grid} 2

trans-unit.source: In the **Processing Toolbox**, now search again for the tool **Zonal Statistics** and open it.
trans-unit.target: In the **Processing Toolbox**, now search again for the tool **Zonal Statistics** and open it.

trans-unit.source: **Raster layer:** `tcd_pop_2025_CN_100m_R2025A_v1.tif`
trans-unit.target: **Raster layer:** `tcd_pop_2025_CN_100m_R2025A_v1.tif`

trans-unit.source: **Vector layer containing zones:** `adm2_access_vaccination_points`
trans-unit.target: **Vector layer containing zones:** `adm2_access_vaccination_points`

trans-unit.source: **Statistics to calculate:** `Sum`
trans-unit.target: **Statistics to calculate:** `Sum`

trans-unit.source: **Output column prefix:** `pop_vaccination_`
trans-unit.target: **Output column prefix:** `pop_vaccination_`

trans-unit.source: **Output file name:** `population_within_isochrones`
trans-unit.target: **Output file name:** `population_within_isochrones`

trans-unit.source: Now we have calculated the total population located within the 2-hour travel-time access area (plus the buffered area), representing the population that can be reached for vaccination within each district.
trans-unit.target: Now we have calculated the total population located within the 2-hour travel-time access area (plus the buffered area), representing the population that can be reached for vaccination within each district.

trans-unit.source: In a final step we will estimate the number of people living more than 2 hours away from the nearest vaccination facility — a key metric for identifying areas where **mobile vaccination teams** or **temporary outreach sites** may be needed.
trans-unit.target: In a final step we will estimate the number of people living more than 2 hours away from the nearest vaccination facility — a key metric for identifying areas where **mobile vaccination teams** or **temporary outreach sites** may be needed.

trans-unit.source: Open the Attribute table and access the Field Calculator
trans-unit.target: Open the Attribute table and access the Field Calculator

trans-unit.source: Give the Output field name `pop_beyond_2h`
trans-unit.target: Give the Output field name `pop_beyond_2h`

trans-unit.source: Output field type will be `Decimal Number (real)`
trans-unit.target: Output field type will be `Decimal Number (real)`

trans-unit.source: And the expression
trans-unit.target: And the expression

trans-unit.source: % THIS IS STILL MISSING
trans-unit.target: % THIS IS STILL MISSING

trans-unit.source: Task 4: Visualising Accessibility
trans-unit.target: Task 4: Visualising Accessibility

trans-unit.source: To visualize the `population_within_isochrones` data we need to join it back onto the original admin 2 boundaries to have the original geometry. Open the __Join attributes by field value__ tool:
trans-unit.target: To visualize the `population_within_isochrones` data we need to join it back onto the original admin 2 boundaries to have the original geometry. Open the __Join attributes by field value__ tool:

trans-unit.source: **Input layer:** `tcd_admbnda_adm2_20250212_AB.shp`
trans-unit.target: **Input layer:** `tcd_admbnda_adm2_20250212_AB.shp`

trans-unit.source: **Table field:** `ADM2_PCODE`
trans-unit.target: **Table field:** `ADM2_PCODE`

trans-unit.source: **Input layer 2:** `population_within_isochrones`
trans-unit.target: **Input layer 2:** `population_within_isochrones`

trans-unit.source: **Table field 2:** `ADM2_PCODE`
trans-unit.target: **Table field 2:** `ADM2_PCODE`

trans-unit.source: **Layer 2 fields to copy:** `pop_total_sum`, `pop_vaccination_sum`, `pop_beyond_2h`
trans-unit.target: **Layer 2 fields to copy:** `pop_total_sum`, `pop_vaccination_sum`, `pop_beyond_2h`

trans-unit.source: **Output file name:** `population_vaccination_adm2`
trans-unit.target: **Output file name:** `population_vaccination_adm2`

trans-unit.source: Now we can visualise both the population that can be reached within 2 hours of a vaccination point and the population living beyond this 2-hour accessibility range.
trans-unit.target: Now we can visualise both the population that can be reached within 2 hours of a vaccination point and the population living beyond this 2-hour accessibility range.

trans-unit.source: Symbolise the population within 2 hours
trans-unit.target: Symbolise the population within 2 hours

trans-unit.source: Select the layer `population_vaccination_adm2` in the Layers panel.
trans-unit.target: Select the layer `population_vaccination_adm2` in the Layers panel.

trans-unit.source: Open the Layer Styling panel and switch to `Graduated` symbology.
trans-unit.target: Open the Layer Styling panel and switch to `Graduated` symbology.

trans-unit.source: Use the following inputs: `pop_vaccination_sum`, Color ramp: RdYlGn (find it under All Color Ramps), Mode: Equal Count, Classes: 5
trans-unit.target: Use the following inputs: `pop_vaccination_sum`, Color ramp: RdYlGn (find it under All Color Ramps), Mode: Equal Count, Classes: 5

trans-unit.source: Symbolise the population beyond 2 hours
trans-unit.target: Symbolise the population beyond 2 hours

trans-unit.source: <kbd>Right-click</kbd> the `population_vaccination_adm2` layer → Duplicate Layer.
trans-unit.target: <kbd>Right-click</kbd> the `population_vaccination_adm2` layer → Duplicate Layer.

trans-unit.source: Open the Layer Styling panel for the duplicate layer and set: Symbology: Graduated, Value: pop_beyond_2h, Color ramp: YlOrRd, Mode: Equal Count, Classes: 5
trans-unit.target: Open the Layer Styling panel for the duplicate layer and set: Symbology: Graduated, Value: pop_beyond_2h, Color ramp: YlOrRd, Mode: Equal Count, Classes: 5

trans-unit.source: Example map for Measles Vaccination: Population Wihin 2-Hour Access | Chad :::
trans-unit.target: Example map for Measles Vaccination: Population Wihin 2-Hour Access | Chad :::

trans-unit.source: Discussion
trans-unit.target: Discussion

trans-unit.source: The resulting map shows which districts and settlements are effectively served by fixed vaccination points, and which remain outside practical reach. In real-world scenarios, such analysis can help humanitarian planners:
trans-unit.target: The resulting map shows which districts and settlements are effectively served by fixed vaccination points, and which remain outside practical reach. In real-world scenarios, such analysis can help humanitarian planners:

trans-unit.source: **Identify underserved areas** and plan **mobile vaccination campaigns**.
trans-unit.target: **Identify underserved areas** and plan **mobile vaccination campaigns**.

trans-unit.source: **Estimate resource needs** for outreach teams.
trans-unit.target: **Estimate resource needs** for outreach teams.
